title: Historic Speechwriting
description: Anatomy of landmark speeches: Lincoln, King, Churchill, Mandela.
summary_pt_br: Discursos históricos: começo pequeno, universal, refrão, passado comum, visão. Frases curtas.

scenes.subtitle_en: Anatomy of landmark speeches: Lincoln, King, Churchill, Mandela.
scenes.subtitle_pt: Discursos históricos: começo pequeno, universal, refrão, passado comum, visão. Frases curtas.

scenes.text_en: Anatomy of landmark speeches: Lincoln, King, Churchill, Mandela. Focus: historic speechwriting. Four score and seven years ago — a refrain that anchors a country.
scenes.text_pt: Discursos históricos: começo pequeno, universal, refrão, passado comum, visão. Frases curtas. Foco: historic speechwriting. Quatro vintenas e sete anos atrás — refrão que ancora um país.

scenes.title: Historic Speechwriting
scenes.body_en: Patterns: OPEN small, BUILD to universal, REPEAT key phrase, CITE common past, CLOSE with vision. Short sentences. Concrete nouns. Silence between lines.
scenes.body_pt: Discursos históricos: começo pequeno, universal, refrão, passado comum, visão. Frases curtas.

examples.en: Four score and seven years ago — a refrain that anchors a country.
examples.pt: Quatro vintenas e sete anos atrás — refrão que ancora um país.

examples.en: 'I have a dream' repeated eight times in one speech.
examples.pt: 'I have a dream' repetido oito vezes.

examples.en: Mandela opened with 'I have cherished' — already choosing forgiveness.
examples.pt: Mandela abriu com 'I have cherished' — já escolhendo perdão.

exercise.question: Complete: "Four score and seven years ago — a ___ that anchors a country."
exercise.explanation: Full sentence: "Four score and seven years ago — a refrain that anchors a country."
exercise.hint_pt_br: Quatro vintenas e sete anos atrás — refrão que ancora um país.

exercise.question: Which sentence is correct?
options: 'I have a dream' repeated eight times in one speech.
I haven't heard anything about that.
exercise.explanation: Correct: "'I have a dream' repeated eight times in one speech."
exercise.hint_pt_br: 'I have a dream' repetido oito vezes.

exercise.explanation: Key terms from the lesson — match each to its meaning.

scenes.title: Historic Speechwriting — dialog
scenes.pt_summary: Diálogo sobre historic speechwriting. Pratique as frases marcadas.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: Kumar, what's common to every great speech?

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: A refrain repeated five or more times.

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: Four score and seven years ago.

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: Free at last, free at last.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: Great speeches rhyme with history, not each other.

scenes.title: The speech class
scenes.pt_summary: Ouça com atenção. Depois, escreva em inglês o que entendeu — resumo, análise, e sua opinião crítica. O professor avaliará.
scenes.prompt_en: Analyze four techniques from a landmark speech. Use at least three speech-writing terms.
scenes.prompt_pt: Escreva em inglês o que ouviu. Análise crítica com quem falou, argumentos, e sua reflexão.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.en: In Kumar's advanced writing class, students studied four landmark speeches side by side on the whiteboard.

paragraphs.speaker: Kumar
paragraphs.en: Lincoln's 'four score and seven years ago' is a refrain that anchors the entire speech to shared national memory.

paragraphs.speaker: Student
paragraphs.en: King repeated 'I have a dream' eight times — each repetition raised the emotional stakes another full degree.

paragraphs.speaker: Kumar
paragraphs.en: Mandela opened his trial speech with 'I have cherished' — already choosing forgiveness as his rhetorical foundation.

paragraphs.speaker: Student
paragraphs.en: Churchill used short Anglo-Saxon words in long, rhythmic sentences that felt inevitable as ocean waves.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.en: Historic speeches, Kumar concluded, rhyme with their era — and with the slow architecture of every speech before them.

scenes.title: Recap reading
scenes.passage_en: At Amazing School, the advanced class just finished "Historic Speechwriting". Anatomy of landmark speeches: Lincoln, King, Churchill, Mandela. Four score and seven years ago — a refrain that anchors a country. 'I have a dream' repeated eight times in one speech. Mr. Kumar reminded the students that at this level, reading widely and writing daily are non-negotiable.

check.question: According to Mr. Kumar, what is non-negotiable at this level?
check.explanation: At C-level, wide reading and daily writing are essential.
check.hint_pt_br: Leitura ampla e escrita diária são essenciais no C.

scenes.title: Listen to Bia
scenes.audio_text_en: Hello! I'm Bia, from Praia do Sol. Today in advanced class we studied "Historic Speechwriting". It was demanding but genuinely rewarding. See you next session!

scenes.title: Go deeper — advanced resources
scenes.body_pt: Fontes avançadas pra aprofundar. Leitura regular é o que separa um aluno avançado de um proficiente.
